text:
The selective capture of carbon dioxide in humid environments is a major challenge in environmental chemistry. We show here that by covalent functionalization of the internal porosity of the MOF, IRMOF-74-III, the capture of CO2 at 65% relative humidity could be achieved efficiently and selectively.

Here we report the synthesis, structural characterization and gas adsorption behavior of a series of functionalized derivatives (e.g. -CH3, -NH2, -CH2NHBoc, -CH2NMeBoc, -CH2NH2, -CH2NHMe). 13C CP/MAS NMR showed that CO2 binds chemically to the CH2NH2 and CH2NHMe functionalized MOFs through the formation of carbamic acid species. The adsorption isotherms and dynamic breakthrough curves show that the IRMOF-74-III-CH2NH2 material has a CO2 uptake of 3.2 mmol/g at 800 Torr. Most importantly, the IRMOF-74-III-CH2NH2 material showed high efficiency in removing CO2 from wet nitrogen with a breakthrough time of 610 ± 10 s g⁻¹. The functionalized material showed high stability towards CO2 removal from wet nitrogen without any structural collapse.
explanation: The text is highly technical and data-dense, resembling a real research abstract. While advanced AI can generate similar content, the specific measurements and coherent scientific narrative suggest human authorship is plausible.
